mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth against forward 20-day SPX returns is negative, or t-test of mean returns in above-median RSAFS growth periods shows no significant difference from below-median periods at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 5.0, the average 60-day forward SPX return is non-negative or the probability of a positive return exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 5% and retail sales contract month-over-month, the combination of tight monetary policy and weakening consumer demand creates a negative shock to equity discount rates and earnings, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Among triggered months, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns exceeds 40%, or the average return is not statistically different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions defined by GDP growth above 2% year-over-year, rising industrial production indicates strong economic momentum that lifts equity markets. The same signal reverses in contractions when output growth reflects inventory accumulation rather than genuine demand.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 YoY growth > 2%, the Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month growth against 20-day forward returns is non-positive over any 3-year window, or the average return in above-median INDPRO growth periods is not significantly higher than in below-median periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts provide an early-cycle lead on residential investment and consumer wealth effects; a high level of housing starts signals confidence and economic momentum that translates into positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward SPX return in weeks following above-median HOUST readings is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or the predictive sign flips in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI inflation rate exceeds 3% year-over-year, the erosion of real purchasing power and the associated risk of central bank tightening dominate equity pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In months with CPI YoY > 3%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is non-negative or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent deterioration in labor market conditions, captured by falling payroll employment and rising unemployment, robustly signals a weakening economy that reduces corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month decline and UNRATE month-over-month increase
failure_condition: Among months with simultaneous PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase, the average 20-day forward SPX return is non-negative, or the hit rate is not below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts enter a sustained decline as measured by a falling 6-month moving average, the economy is likely in a late-cycle phase where further GDP growth becomes a sell signal as it may be unsustainable and precede a downturn. GDP growth in this regime predicts negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month moving average declining from its peak over the prior 6 months
regime_hint: late-cycle housing decline (HOUST peaked and falling for 6 months)
failure_condition: In periods when the HOUST 6-month MA is declining and GDPC1 YoY growth is positive, the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or the signal fails to outperform the unconditional mean return.
